name: The Catalogue itself
desc: Built 2026-07-13. The first thing I've ever put live on the internet.

name: Kg-Calibrator
desc: A phone-first tracker that finds my personal calories-per-kg number. Log daily calories in and out; every fourth entry it asks my weight; once it has two weight readings, it tells me exactly how much deficit I need to lose 1 kg — for my body, not a generic formula.

name: Personal Cloud Backend
desc: Self-hosted API and file store running on my own hardware at cloud.atomz.online. Serves data and files for any of my creations, replacing dependence on third-party cloud services. Fully owned stack — no rented servers, no free-tier lock-in.

name: GateCurry
desc: A phone-first fuel and mileage tracker for my Grand i10. Log odometer and liters at each fill-up, and it calculates true km-per-liter, cost-per-km, and average mileage over time. Uses my own cloud backend.

name: Tamper-Evident Audit Log
desc: Every write to my personal cloud backend — across all my apps — is now cryptographically fingerprinted and chained. Uses SHA-256 hashing, HMAC signing, and OpenTimestamps anchoring to Bitcoin so entire history can be verified by anyone, even without trusting me. Comes with a visual admin dashboard that flags any tampered rows in red, and a public 'prove this record' link feature that lets outsiders independently verify any single record's integrity against Bitcoin's timestamped chain — no login required.

name: Product Hunt Intelligence
desc: A personal intelligence platform that pulls Product Hunt launches via the official API, stores them in a dedicated database on my cloud, and analyzes them with rankings, category performance, maker track records, and a transparent PH Score. Not a scraper — a long-term research archive. Currently holds 13,960 products.

name: Notification Service
desc: A universal iPhone notification system built into my personal cloud backend. Any of my creations — current or future — can fire native iOS push notifications through it, using ntfy as the delivery layer. Includes a scheduler for recurring reminders (daily times, intervals, IST-aware), a history log of every notification fired, and integration with my tamper-evident audit chain so notification history is cryptographically verifiable. Zero recurring cost, zero ads, fully self-hosted.
